failure_modes.text: Boiling the ocean can waste effort on comprehensiveness long after the core question is already clear enough to resolve.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a 'good enough' threshold for analysis before opening the prioritization exercise: define when the ranking is clear enough to act and stop refining.

failure_modes.text: Unwarranted optimism can make a complex plan look executable even when too many steps must go right together.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stress-test the top-ranked items against outside-view failure rates: check whether the plan requires too many conjunctive successes to be realistic.

failure_modes.text: Premature filtering can steer the shortlist toward subconscious preferences instead of objective leverage.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use blind ranking or independent scoring before group discussion to prevent anchoring and social-proof effects from steering the shortlist.

premortem_questions.text: How does what you're doing solve the problem, and is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: How does what you’re doing solve the problem? How does it advance your thinking? Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?

premortem_questions.text: Do I really need to be optimizing here, or is the best thing to do simply to eliminate it completely?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Do I really need to be optimizing here, or is the best thing to do simply to eliminate it completely?

heuristics.text: Use the Pareto principle to find the few activities that generate most of the leverage.
Use the Eisenhower matrix to keep urgency from quietly displacing what matters.
Make the work hypothesis-driven and end-product oriented before gathering more analysis.

curation_notes.summary: The raw file has clear elimination, leverage, and anti-ocean-boiling intervention content, so the chunk set keeps those signals explicit.
donor_drops: Ignored donor material that mainly restated prioritization use cases instead of preserving intervention chunks.
open_questions: The source is richer on leverage heuristics than on named failure tables, but the failure content still supports a conservative chunk layer.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems thinking, cognitive load, HDPS, and commander's-intent style alignment.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section gives future relation material around Maslow's hammer, confirmation bias, and curse of knowledge.
structured_tensions: No explicit structured tension block appears here, but the raw anti-pattern material is strong enough for later relation preservation.
